meta.description: Command center — primary operational view. Introduces check-in group as the core operational entity between bookings/queue entries and individual guests.
meta.impact: Replaces service_status as the primary state-tracked entity. Service state machine now lives on check-in groups, not directly on bookings/queue entries.

check_in_group.description: First-class entity. Child of a booking or queue entry. One by default, multiple when split. Each runs the full service state machine independently. The command center displays check-in groups, not raw bookings.

relationship.default: One check-in group created automatically when booking/queue entry becomes active
relationship.split: Operator splits into 2+ groups. Each tracks its own headcount, waivers, service state.
relationship.resolution: When all groups under a parent reach completed, the parent booking/queue entry is complete.

fields.label: string (e.g. 'Group A', 'Late arrivals', operator-editable)
fields.headcount: number (this group's portion of the total)
fields.waiver_count: number (computed from waiver submissions linked to this group)
fields.split_from: string | null (id of group this was split from, for audit trail)

group_status_meanings.active: This group is moving through the service state machine right now
group_status_meanings.waiting: This group is waiting for guests to arrive (split scenario, bunched arrival)
group_status_meanings.fulfilled: This group's headcount was paid for but guests may or may not arrive (short players scenario). Still accepts late arrivals.

service_state_machine.description: Expanded state machine. Includes pre-arrival prep states. Lives on check-in groups, not on bookings directly.

prepped.description: Staff has prepared the area/equipment for this group

photos_taken.description: Toggled independently at any point during service phase. Not a state, just a flag.

service_state_machine.notes: Pre-arrival states are staff-only, no guest interaction. Check-in phase involves guest interaction and waiver gate. Service phase is the activity itself.

split_mechanics.trigger: Operator activates split on a check-in group
split_mechanics.what_happens: The original group's headcount is divided across two new groups. Original group persists as a logical parent or is replaced — TBD.

bunched_arrival.description: Group of 20 arrives in two cars. First 12 are here, 8 are 30 minutes away.
flow: Booking has 1 default check-in group (headcount 20)
Operator splits into Group A (12, active) and Group B (8, waiting)
Group A proceeds: waivers -> checked_in -> safety -> playing
Group B waits. When remaining 8 arrive, operator marks Group B arrived
Group B proceeds through its own state machine independently
When both groups reach completed, parent booking is complete

short_players.description: Group of 15 booked, only 10 show up. Remaining 5 may or may not come.
flow: Operator splits into Group A (10, active) and Group B (5, fulfilled)
Group A proceeds normally through full state machine
Group B is flagged fulfilled — it's paid for, but on hold
If 3 of the 5 eventually arrive: operator adjusts Group B headcount to 3, collects waivers + wristbands, advances Group B
If nobody comes: Group B stays fulfilled, closed by day-close routine
The 2 no-shows are just absorbed — paid headcount doesn't change, arrived headcount is less
No split needed
Operator adjusts headcount on the single check-in group from 10 to 12
Additional 2 pay at POS
Waivers collected for all 12
Proceeds normally
extra_players.notes: Headcount adjustment on check-in group triggers a discrepancy with parent booking paid_headcount. POS payment covers the difference. System tracks: parent paid_headcount=10, group arrived_headcount=12, pos_additional=2.

late_arrival_after_active.description: Group is already playing. 2 people show up late.
flow: If a fulfilled/waiting split exists: operator moves late arrivals into that group, collects waiver + wristband, advances them
If no split exists: operator creates a new check-in group (headcount 2, split_from original)
Late arrival group has a compressed state machine — may skip prepped/filled since area is already set up
Waiver + wristband still required
late_arrival_after_active.open_question: Can a check-in group skip states, or does it always start at prepped? For late arrivals, prepped/filled are already done by definition.

resolution.description: A split is resolved when all child groups under the parent booking are completed.
resolution.visual: Once resolved, command center shows the booking as a single completed line item again.
resolution.data: All check-in group records persist for audit/reporting. Resolution is a computed state, not a stored one.

command_center_view.description: Split view. Bookings (Track 1) on left, queue (Track 2) on right. Displays check-in groups, not raw bookings. When a booking has 1 group, it looks like a booking. When split, it expands to show child groups.

left_panel.title: Bookings
left_panel.content: Today's online bookings, ordered by time slot. Each booking shows its check-in group(s).
left_panel.collapsed_view: Booking line: time, name, package, headcount, service state indicator, waiver indicator, photos indicator
left_panel.expanded_view: When split: booking header + indented child groups, each with own state/waiver/headcount

right_panel.title: Queue
right_panel.content: Today's queue entries by priority. Each shows its check-in group (usually 1:1).
right_panel.collapsed_view: Queue line: priority tier badge, name, party size, status, service state indicator

operator: Advance service state on any check-in group
editor: Adjust headcount beyond original booking (without POS — corrections)

drill_downs./command-center/booking/[id]: Booking detail with all check-in groups, waiver status, order link
drill_downs./command-center/queue/[id]: Queue entry detail with check-in group, waiver status
drill_downs./command-center/group/[id]: Check-in group detail — headcount, waiver list, service state timeline, split history

command_center_view.date_nav: Same DateNavigator component. ?date= param. Default today.

impact_on_existing_architecture.service_status_table: Replaced by check_in_group. Service state now lives on the group, not a separate table.
impact_on_existing_architecture.booking_status: Booking lifecycle (waiting, active, skipped, complete, gift_certificate) still exists on the booking itself. But the operational state is on check-in groups.
impact_on_existing_architecture.queue_entry_status: Same — queue lifecycle still exists. Operational state on check-in groups.
impact_on_existing_architecture.waiver_association: Waivers now associate to check_in_group_id, not directly to booking_id. The token in the waiver link maps to a group. For pre-split bookings, maps to the default group.

routes.modified: /bookings and /queue still exist as standalone views for editors reviewing data, but operators primarily use command center
routes.relationship: /bookings and /queue are data views. /command-center is the operational view.

open_questions.state_skipping: Can late-arrival check-in groups skip prepped/filled? Or do those states get auto-advanced?
open_questions.split_original_group: When splitting, does the original group get replaced by two new groups, or does it persist with children?
open_questions.waiver_redistribution: When a split happens after some waivers are already submitted, how do waivers redistribute across groups? By name? Operator assigns?
open_questions.headcount_accounting: Need clear model for: parent booking paid_headcount vs. sum of group headcounts vs. sum of group waiver counts vs. POS additional payments
open_questions.queue_splits: Can queue entries also be split, or is this Track 1 only? Large walk-up groups could have the same problem.
